title: Creating a table with duplicate column name should not be possible
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE products (id int not null primary key, name varchar(99) not null, price decimal(7,2) not null);
CREATE TABLE products_duplicate (id int not null primary key, name1 varchar(99) not null, name2 varchar(99) not null, price decimal(7,2) not null);
INSERT INTO products (id, name, price) VALUES (1, 'apple', 0.99);
INSERT INTO products_duplicate (id, name1, name2, price) VALUES (2, 'orange', 'orange', 1.29);
SELECT id, name1, name2, price FROM products_duplicate;
key_bug_features: Creating tables with duplicate column names allowed
